entry.title: PDFs are sent to external Printer in Image PDF format, not text readable
entry.content:
Team,

We wanted to automate the printing process of few documents to send to external printer(server).

We are receiving the base64 format of the PDF files from an external application to SAP with OData.

We have converted this to binary and used below ADS function modules to convert to PDF spool and send to printer.

 ADS_SR_OPEN,  ADS_GET_PATH ,  ADS_SR_CONFIRM 

But, these PDF are sent to external printer/server as PDF Images, text on them is not readable.

When PDF is opened from SP01, then the text on PDFs is selectable and readable, but only when it reached external printer/server, they are Image PDFs.

When we saw the spool properties, they are GRAW format. I think this could be reason the issue.

We have given PDF1 as Device Type and U(Print using Berkley protocol) as access method to send to external printer.

What could be the reason for this issue? How can we solve this issue?

entry.title: Top 10 SAP Cloud ALM News January 2025
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM continues to evolve with new features that enhance project management, monitoring, and traceability.

Here are the top 10 highlights from the latest January 2025 update:

1. Job & Automation Monitoring:

New filters in the Analysis page to refine jobs and automation searches by Step/Executable, Executed By, and Scheduled By.

 2. Quality Gates and Requirements:

Spreadsheet uploads can now assign quality gates to project tasks and requirements to user stories.

3. Conflict Resolution Information:

Detailed conflict resolution information is available in the Check Details view.

 4. Checklist Management:
Teams can now be assigned to checklist items for better task accountability.

5. Quality Gate Status Filter:

A new filter for quality gate statuses has been added to the app.

6. Custom Solution Process Management:

Introduced a two-step deletion process using archiving to prevent accidental data deletion. Processes can be archived and later restored or permanently deleted.

7. Event Action Configuration:

Event Name and Service Type are now viewable in the event action configuration screen. Live Search can be used to find variants by these details.

 

8. Transport Check Results:

You can download check results of conflicting or missing objects in a single spreadsheet.

 

9. Test Case Images:

Inserted images in test cases are retained upon downloading and re-uploading the test cases.

10.System Groups Search:

In the System Groups tab, you can now search by system title or ID in the selection list for easier navigation.

 

These top 10 features in the January 2025 SAP Cloud ALM update are designed to streamline operations, improve management efficiency, and enhance user experience.

For more detailed information, visit the SAP Help Portal.

Stay tuned for more exciting updates as SAP continues to innovate and improve the #Cloud ALM experience

entry.title: Re: Group number is defaulted when using Routing Master a...
entry.content: The Configuration Activity 'Assign Number Ranges to Task List Type Routing' 102738 has the customizing flag "TL" to control whether the default group number is used

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Trusted Authorization Review (STAR)
entry.content:
Hola, 

Hice uso del servicio y lo recomiendo 100%.  Pude entender más sobre los FUE y como hacer la equivalencia; no es simple y tuvimos hallazgos importantes como que no solo se debe analizar a nivel de transacción y objetos de autorización, sino que se debe analizar también a nivel de actividad.  Vimos casos de usuarios que en general son del tipo 'visualizadores' y debido a que tenía una actividad 'X' (no recuerdo el número ahora)  o incluso actividad * implicaba que ese usuario iba a consumir 1 FUE (uso avanzado). 

Entonces les recomiendo el servicio y graben para revisar nuevamente las recomendaciones y el paso a paso que deben realizar para en análisis.

Como resultado, vemos que se genera pre proyectos de análisis y optimización de roles antes o durante la migración, y luego vemos también que deberá ser una tarea permanente durante la operación SAP S/4HANA.  Mucha tarea para el oficial de seguridad SAP.

Así, sería ideal que SAP pudiera automatizar este análisis en el portal me.sap.com, porque el procedimiento es muy manual y con trabajo en excel. Util, pero requiere dedicación

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports XI upgrade path to SAP Crystal Report...
entry.content:
Hello,

As of CR 9 SAP stopped including the .NET SDK with Crystal Reports. You can get it from here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

It integrates into VS 2010->2022 and above. Note: Run the EXE to install on your DEV PC to integrate into VS, all other files are for distribution only, not required to install on you DEV PC, and install the 32 or 64 bit runtime when it asks at the end of the install, both are required for Development.

See my Blog on upgrading your Project:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/upgrading-a-visual-studio-2008-gt-2022-net-project-with-the-latest-cr-net/ba-p/13469215

And NO, you don't need to upgrade your reports, it is recommended to bring them to current versions though but mostly not required. Some DB Clients need to be updated so in those cases you do need to update your reports.

The differences between the full version of CRD and the Embedded version in VS is the VS version uses the old CRAXDDRT.DLL, the Embedded Report Designer, but it does have limitations built into it, also known as CR Basic... it does upgrade to current version of the report.

You can get the history of the Report versions using the SDK.

Create a History Box and use this to get the info:

cbLastSaveHistory.Text = "";
try
{
    // Read File Details from CFileInfo Object
    //cbLastSaveHistory.Sorted = true; 

for (int x = 0; x < rpt.HistoryInfos.Count; x++)
{
    cbLastSaveHistory.Items.Add(rpt.HistoryInfos[x].BuildVersion.ToString() + ": Date: " + rpt.HistoryInfos[x].SavedDate.ToString());
}

cbLastSaveHistory.SelectedIndex = 0; 
    // rpt.HistoryInfos.Count - 1;
    //SP 13
    //•	RAS .NET SDK 
    //ReportClientDocument.HistoryInfos[i].SavedDate
    //ReportClientDocument.HistoryInfos[i].BuildVersion

    //•	CR .NET SDK 
    //ReportDocument.HistoryInfos[i].SavedDate
    //ReportDocument.HistoryInfos[i].BuildVersion
}
catch (Exception ex)
{
    //MessageBox.Show("Error: " + ex.Message);
    //MessageBox.Show("This report has no Save history");
    cbLastSaveHistory.Text = "This report has no Save history";
}

The output looks like this:

So what you should do is:

1. Install CR 2020

2. Install CR for VS SP 37, latest version

3. Open each CR XI report in CR 2020 and Set Location to the DB and run the report to verify it is working and then save the Report to a new location, or backup your originals.

4. Upgrade your Project, FYI, 32 bit runtime is end of life this coming December so set your Project to X64 and not AnyCPU.

Have fun

Don

entry.title: Revealing Hidden SAP Business Network Configurations: Insights for Delivery Consultants
entry.content:
This blog details configurations on SAP Business Network accessible exclusively to SAP Customer Support or Services teams.

It’s tailored for delivery consultants seeking insight into these behind-the-scenes settings.

Key Configuration Principles

  1. Initial Setup
    The provisioning team configures the buyer’s account based on the purchased license.
  2. Account-Specific Configuration
    Each buyer’s account has unique configurations for test, production, and sometimes development accounts."
  3. Cut-Over Process
    At go-live, configurations are manually migrated from test to production, one at a time.

Let’s start from entitlements. This is a set of options that is mostly set based on default values and most of them. Below table represents the breakdown of options:

Download PO Confirmations

Download Ship Notices

Download PO Statuses

Download Notifications

First four options are used if your customer has a custom integration directly with Network and not through CIG. I’ll explore reasons for not using CIG in a future post.

These entitlements ensure SAP Ariba Buying updates Purchase Orders with OC and ASN info. Disabling them accidentally can disrupt visibility for requesters and purchasing teams.

Upload CSV Payment Proposals / Payment Batch

This feature is always enabled by default. Although rarely used, it’s harmless to keep active.

Sign all Invoices with Esignature

This setting is often enabled by the provisioning team, but I’ve seen instances where it wasn’t. For European customers with invoicing in scope, enabling this is mandatory to ensure compliance. Without it, EU invoices lack the required e-signature tag in the cXML.

Label both CXML and PDF invoice documents as ‘Tax Invoice’ for Australia and New Zealand

This setting applies only to Australia and New Zealand. For other countries, customers often request customizations to apply this label to PDF invoices.

Download Supplier Data

 

This legacy configuration is disabled by default.

Send VendorID,SideID and SiteAuxID in outgoing documents

It may apply in non-SAP ERP setups with multiple backends for a customer using SAP Ariba Buying.

Allow Multiple Private ID’s per Supplier

This is for legacy Invoice Conversion Service (ICS), which is no longer actively sold by SAP. It remains enabled by default and poses no risk.

Add missing vendor records based on vendor compound keys in documents

This functionality is unused, and no documentation is available.

Allow Supplier to enter service details for OC

This setting relates to Service POs and their order confirmations. Learn more: See more here: https://support.ariba.com/item/view/KB0407735

Copy accounting information from parent line in purchase order to child lines in Service Sheet

This applies only when accounting is included in the Purchase Order.

Allow suppliers to create invoice directly from service Pos without a service sheet

This is used when customers bypass SES approval to allow suppliers to create service invoices immediately.

For SAP Ariba Buying customers, refer to https://support.ariba.com/item/view/KB0405882

Allow Buyer to activate Supply Chain Collaboartion features

This setting depends on the licenses purchased by the customer from SAP.

Allow suppliers to exclude withoholding tax from Gross Amount

This applies to Latin American countries using third-party integration platforms (e.g., EDICOM) or integrated suppliers sending invoices. Learn more: https://support.ariba.com/item/view/KB0395328

Enhanced order confirmation integration capabilities

This setting resolves multiple issues, including this common one: https://connectsupport.ariba.com/sites#item-view&/178098

 

Allow integration with SAP S/4HANA for central procurement

 

Enabled when Central Procurement is in scope.

Enforce Control Keys for All Suppliers

When enabled, control keys on purchase orders set tolerances for suppliers creating documents. If disabled, Default Transaction Rules apply, and without tolerance configuration, suppliers have no restrictions.

 

Ignore DUNS credential when a matching supplier does not have a relationship with the buyer 

see this for more info https://itsm.services.sap/ kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0396609

 

Send shipping cost in invoice item, if order item has the shipping amount

This entitlement is rarely used and lacks documentation. It may relate to the total landed cost feature in SAP Ariba Buying, where shipping costs from the Purchase Order are copied to the invoice.

Require Italian invoice to flow to SDI

For details on the Italian invoice flow, refer to: https://support.ariba.com/item/view/181173

Allow multiple buyer user admins and mass user creation

self explanotroy

Enable document tracking on ship notices

Enable BN4L integration for Ship notices

 

Enabled when Business Network for Logistics solutions are in scope.

In addition to entitlements, there are several other functions that may need to be enabled, including:

  • This feature is typically enabled by default but may be missed during manual provisioning. Key functions include:
  • Providing visibility for default transaction rules, such as 'Copy tax from PO to invoice.'
  • Supporting payment request messages and functionality.
  • Enabling integration with non-SAP ERP systems or non-CIG-based integrations.

Another important configuration:

and:

End point support option enables end point configuration to appear in the administration menu. Almost always is turned on by default for all customers using Guided Buying.

How does end point configuration works is explained in another blog post.

Enablement for pending queue is used in integration with non-SAP systems, when the customer decides to use integration with ‘getpending queue’ method.

Next we have:

This setting is required for ERP-initiated invoice functionality. Learn more::

https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-q-a/dsn-erp-initiated-invoice-not-visible-to-supplier/qaq-p/12369910

Next one that is disabled by default:

This functionality, disabled by default, works as described. However, based on personal experience, enabling it for SAP backends can cause issues with processing attachments after invoice posting. This is not supported for API but only for Portal.

This critical functionality is usually configured by the SBN Services team, but it’s important to verify during deployment.

Turn on PDF Generation for organization

 

This option enables the automatic generation of a human-readable copy of an invoice when it is archived.

Generate an invoice copy as PDF attachment to the invoice

Allow for the invoice to generate an attachment, visible in the attachments section at the bottom of the invoice screen. If a relevant business rule is enabled, this attachment can also be passed to an SAP or ERP backend. Some customers prefer having this document in their ERP system.

 

In one case, a customer printed all auto-generated PDFs for physical storage to meet audit requirements, using the paper invoice as the legal document instead of the electronic (cXML) version.

Generate an invoice copy as PDF attachment to the reconciled invoice

This functionality is applicable only to legacy Invoice Pro or Invoice Management customers.

Enable FPA PDF generation for Italy

This functionality includes features specific to Italy for compliance purposes.

Next setting worth knowing about:

The default attachment size is 10MB, but it can be increased to 100MB and often is by SAP Services Team as part of best practices configuration. This is particularly relevant for customers directly integrated with the Network.

Next functionality:

This feature, relevant for the few remaining 'ICS' customers, enables sending invoice PDFs that create INV-type documents in SAP Ariba Buying. Partially captured documents with some fields populated and others containing dummy values can also be sent.

Moving forward we have:

Enable this if Sourcing customers want to invite suppliers from the Discovery platform to their Sourcing events. Learn more:

https://help.sap.com/docs/strategic-sourcing/managing-events-with-guided-sourcing/creating-sap-business-network-discovery-postings-in-guided-sourcing?locale=en-US

Next in line is quote automation. This configuration is necessary to send RFQs from SAP to the Discovery platform.

Other features not covered in this blog post are related to SSO configuration that is possible on SAP Business Network for buyers. Also PDF templates configuration is not covered in this post. This means PO custom template but also Ship Notice, Handling Unit and Quality Inspection templates.

entry.title: ATC Check variants SAP_CP_READINESS vs ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS: which one to use?
entry.content:
Hi all,

eg in a current ABAP Environment there are the ATC check variants SAP_CP_READINESS and ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS available.

What is the expected difference of those? Which one should we use in the future?

They appear to be mostly identical, one subtle difference is in the parameters of the check CI_NO_ORDER_BY "Reads assuming sortedness for SELECTs without ORDER BY":

  • AnalyzeOnlyLocalProcedures is true for ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS, false for SAP_CP_READINESS
  • NonLocalAnalysisDepth is 1 for ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS, 2 for SAP_CP_READINESS

Thanks,

  Wolfgang

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Parameter Asking for Login Information
entry.content:
Hi Greg,

Ah, FireDAC must have a Client Database connector that CR uses to connect to it.

CR 2016 is a 32 bit application and CR 2020 is a 64 bit application so it requires a 64 bit Client.

The way CR works is when the DB connection is made CR stores the log on info in memory and releases the connection so the Client doesn't get hit with potentially 100's of connection requests. Normal behaviour...

I suspect what FireDAC is doing is releasing the connection, so each CR parameter will open the connection to pass the parameter to the Client/DB. FireDAC appears to be closing each connection. Because CR Parameters must be set before the connection is made, it's like a Stored Procedure with parameters, the value must be set before the log on info is attempted.

If you enable CRLogger, KBA 1603398 - How to trace data source connections in Crystal Reports using crlogger? [Video]

The log file may show what is happening...

I suggest contacting FireDAC support and ask them if they have a 64 bit Client or if they could have a look at your issue, the log file may help them determine what is happening and why the connection is lost every time a refresh is completed.

Nothing in CR configuration to make this work, no difference between CR 2016 and CR 2020 has changed for this work flow...

Don

entry.title: Re: Master database creation failed for SAP ASE 16.0 SP03...
entry.content:
According to SAPNote 2489781 and SAPNote 2531326, SuSE 15.5 should be supported.  (There is also a hyperscaler entrypoint SAPNote 2922454 with additional information and links).

However, SAPNote 3018138 also states that the error you may be receiving is fixed in SP03PL11 and higher.  I would suggest moving fully to SP03PL15 (if remaining on SP03 is the current priority) as it is the last PL of SP03.  If the problem is not fixed then, please open a support incident ASAP.

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite – Access Policies for Integration Packages
entry.content:
Hi @PeterGutsche, 

is there any way to combine Access Policies with restrictions on the actions (editing, deleting, configuring,...) on Integration Artifacts which can be performed?

I know that there is the role WorkspacePackagesConfigure which only gives permissions for configuring. But can we somehow intersect permissions from this role with the ones from an Access Policy?

entry.title: February 2025 Mobile App Updates question
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP recently shared information about February 2025 Mobile App Updates / New iOS Version & Multi-Profile Changes. In February iOS app users will be required to sign in again. What if users use the devices with SSO where they are logged in automatically, do you know if it will require any action for them?

Many thanks for your insight.

Br,
Vaiva

February 2025 Changes

Some important changes are coming to the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app in February 2025. One change will affect all iOS users, and another set of changes will affect the relatively small number of users who share a single device with others (Multi-Profile Mode).

iOS App Transfer

This change will impact all iOS users who update the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app to the February release.

In January, as part of a consolidation across SAP, the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile iOS app will transfer from the SuccessFactors, Inc. account to the SAP SE account. The February iOS app release will be the first under the SAP SE account, and anyone who updates the iOS app will be required to sign in again.

We know being asked to sign in again can be frustrating, so please consider informing your employees and support staff of this coming event.

Try removing the @ from the formula name.

Never use the Shift top row characters on your keyboard in the names of anything, those Characters are typically reserved for internal use.

Also, for Command Parameters you should be using them in the Command UI:

There could be an issue with the MS Client you are using, sometimes MS OLE DB Client works and sometimes not...

As a test ONLY, create a 64 ODBC DSN to your SQL Server and see if that works?

entry.title: Re: Intent-based Navigation - semanticObjectAction annota...
entry.content:
hi @RaminS in the second link you can find the following information: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/d782acf8bfd74107ad6a04f0361c5f62.html

Navigation to a Semantic Object (Intent-Based Navigation)

in section "Using a Button" you can see that type: #FOR_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION is used to render a button

in section "Using a Link" you can see that type: #WITH_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION is used to render it as a link and it is supposed to be used with LineItems

You can also find general information about the popover: When a user chooses the link, and only one navigation target is found, direct navigation to the target is triggered. If more than one target is found, the system displays a popover containing some text and links to the targets for the user to choose from

So if you have a LineItem i.e. column in a table and you want to render it as a link you choose #WITH_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION. If the combination of semanticObject and action is unique a direct navigation is triggered.

entry.title: Re: Sending data in excel file from SAP to a shared folde...
entry.content:
To send data in an Excel file from SAP to a shared folder on a virtual machine, you can try the following approach:

  1. Ensure Network Accessibility: First, make sure that the virtual machine’s shared folder is accessible from the SAP system. Check network connectivity and permissions.

  2. Use SAP Function Modules for File Handling: You can use SAP function modules like GUI_UPLOAD or OPEN_DATA to handle Excel file data, but to write to a shared network location, consider using a different approach.

  3. Use the FTP or SMB Protocol: If the virtual machine is configured to allow FTP or SMB (Windows file sharing), you can use function modules like FTP_CONNECT, FTP_COMMAND to send the file. Ensure the destination folder is accessible with the correct credentials.

  4. Check User Permissions: Make sure the SAP system has the required access rights to write files to the shared folder. This may involve setting up proper credentials for SAP to access the virtual machine.

  5. Background Job or Process: For automation, you can set up a background job in SAP to periodically export the Excel file and move it to the shared folder using FTP or network paths.

  6. Verify the RFC Connection: Since you already have an RFC connection established, ensure it is functioning properly. Sometimes, issues with network settings or misconfigured parameters can block file transfers.

entry.title: Re: Good afternoon dear, I have a question, I always run...
entry.content:
Hi

To exclude sales orders and offers from the MRP planning in MD02 there are a few options, but the most relevant one is configuring the MRP Type and adjusting the settings so that sales orders and offers are not considered during the planning process.

Check MRP Type Settings:
In MD02, the MRP run takes into account specific MRP types that determine which elements are considered for planning. You can modify the settings for the MRP type to exclude sales orders and offers.

Go to Transaction SPRO → Production → Material Requirements Planning → MRP → Define MRP Types.
Here, check the settings for the MRP type used in your materials. Select the MRP type that not consider sale order. 

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Sapphire: Designing great content for every attendee.
entry.content:
Each year SAP Sapphire brings together thousands of business and IT leaders from around the world to network with their peers and to discover and learn all about the latest SAP solutions and innovations. The event brings together many audiences, with new and existing customers, ecosystem partners, and people in a diverse array of roles and industries. How does SAP ensure the sessions and demos at every SAP Sapphire offer the maximum value for everyone?

It is my privilege to lead the team that designs the content program for SAP Sapphire. Experts from every solution area work together to design the content program ‘audience-first’. Each year we make sure we plan sessions that achieve specific attendee goals. We make sure we have a balanced program with a strong set of content for every key audience segment. We look for the best speakers, hosts, and experts. And we look at data and feedback from previous events to make sure we deliver more of what our audience wants. Let’s explore each of these topics in a little more detail.

 

Sessions that meet attendee goals

We've all experienced conferences that didn't quite hit the mark – where the host company stuck to the typical 'default' format. There were too many PowerPoint presentations, a few demo booths, and a keynote that went on a bit longer than expected. In the audience, we found ourselves waiting for the next break in the agenda. At SAP, we focus on avoiding the pitfalls of 'default' thinking by asking a simple question: why? Why would someone choose to attend a particular session? This approach has allowed us to evolve our event, ensuring that every type of content we offer is specifically designed to meet the unique needs of our attendees.

 

With audience needs driving all our planning, we make sure to deliver the maximum possible value for attendees. But this is not the only benefit. It is also a lot easier for the SAP teams to plan an interesting mix and variety of sessions on each solution or topic. Understanding audience needs helps our hosts and presenters to prepare the right session materials and deliver a consistent experience. And understanding the goal of each content type helps our attendees focus on what matters most to them. We use plain English names like ‘Ask the Expert’ for the session types at the event to make this even easier.

 

Balancing the needs of many audiences

SAP Sapphire in Orlando features more than 600 sessions and demos. In order to see all of them you would have to build a time machine and attend the event 40 times. This is obviously not practical. It is also not necessary, because not all 600 sessions and demos are relevant to the same people. Like most events, the content program at SAP Sapphire is split into tracks – each focused on a specific solution or audience. But each track is sized and designed based on a number of important criteria.

  • Popularity – how many people went to sessions on this topic at the last event? More popular tracks get more sessions. A popular track could get as many as 10% of the sessions. Others could get 5% or less. But even with 5% you would need a time machine to see all of them. So other criteria are also important.
  • Maturity – the mix of session types for a track will depend on how new the topic is, or how many customers have deployed the solution. Newer topics or solutions get more high-level sessions, like strategy talks and demos. More established topics get more deep-dive content like Ask the Expert sessions. We aim to give people the information they need.
  • Variety – regardless of the topic, nobody likes repetitive content. So, our experts plan sessions and demos which cover many different perspectives and roles. And sessions which appeal to customers at different stages of their journey – from just learning about a solution, to trying to maximize ROI for a live system.

As a final step, once the team’s content plans have been drafted, we ask them to look at the event through every audience lens possible. To make sure that people from every audience we expect at the event will have plenty of sessions and demos to choose from. Plenty of opportunities to discover, learn, and network.

 

Finding the best speakers, hosts, and experts

SAP Sapphire is a community event. It is not just for and about SAP. It is also for and about our customers and partners. So, every year we work to make sure that our customers’ voices and our partners’ voices are heard at the event. Our goal is to make sure that 50% of the voices you hear in sessions at SAP Sapphire are those of our customers or partners. So, every year the content design team is asked to look for the customers and partners doing the most important and innovative things with SAP solutions – so we can invite them to share their stories at the conference.

And every year we try to make sure that the SAP speakers our customers and partners want to learn from are on-site and on stage. Our goal in selecting SAP speakers and hosts is to find the people our customers and partners most want to meet and talk with – product leaders, technical experts, solution experts, industry experts, and more.

 

Data-driven decisions

Woven into all of the content team’s planning is data. In particular, data from our most recent SAP Sapphire events. We know how many people scanned their badges at each demo station. We know approximately how many people attended every session, because of passive scanners in each theater. Collectively, this data informs us of the interests of each audience – and how engaging our content was for them. With data from many past events, we can also see how the audience and their interests are evolving.

We hope you will join us for SAP Sapphire in 2025 in either Orlando or Madrid. If you do, you can help. Make sure to opt in to tracking, so you are counted as an attendee in the sessions you join. The data is anonymized and aggregated for analysis – we don’t track individuals. And please respond to the post-event survey to share your feedback. Tell us what you liked or didn’t like. What you want more of, or less. In return my team and I will keep working to plan better, more relevant, more engaging, and more valuable content – optimized for your needs – every time.

entry.title: Layouts Básicos e Floorplans do SAP Fiori
entry.content:
O layout básico do Fiori é o layout dinâmico (dynamic layout). Ele é dividido em cabeçalho, página e rodapé. Essa divisão ajuda o usuário a focar no conteúdo da página.

Outro tipo de layout é o de colunas flexíveis. Com ele, é possível exibir diferentes floorplans em uma única página, o que proporciona uma navegação mais rápida e fluida em comparação ao layout dinâmico, permitindo uma divisão de até 10 colunas.

Os floorplans, sejam eles pré-definidos ou customizados, podem ser encaixados nos layouts mencionados acima.

Dentre os floorplans que melhor se encaixam nos layouts, destacam-se:

  • Overview Page: Um aplicativo que exibe todas as informações que o usuário precisa em uma página, com base em seu perfil.

  • List Report: Permite aos usuários trabalhar com um grande volume de dados, onde é possível realizar filtros de busca e ações nos itens exibidos. Geralmente, é utilizado como porta de entrada para a exibição detalhada de um item em uma Object Page.

  • Object Page: Utilizado para exibir e categorizar informações de um único objeto, como uma ordem de venda. As informações podem ser acessadas por âncoras (links) ou abas.

  • Wizard: Permite ao usuário completar uma tarefa longa em seções. Consiste em um passo a passo onde cada parte do formulário é revelada a cada passo completado. Ao final, há uma página de resumo que exibe todos os dados preenchidos pelo usuário, prontos para submissão.

  • Initial Page: Permite navegar para um determinado registro ou editá-lo. O ponto inicial é um campo único exibido.

  • Worklist: Exibe uma coleção de itens que o usuário precisa processar. Permite revisar os itens da lista e tomar ações, como completar uma tarefa ou delegar para outro usuário.

  • Analytical Page (ALP): Oferece uma maneira única de analisar dados passo a passo sob diferentes perspectivas. Serve para identificar dados interessantes, já que os gráficos são interativos e filtráveis entre si. Por fim, permite agir em um registro específico.

entry.title: 3-System Landscape Conversion in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | Expert Talk
entry.content:
In a recent Expert Talk video, SAP experts and industry leaders gathered to discuss the strategic importance and technical nuances of transitioning from a 2-system to a 3-system landscape in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This blog post aims to distill the key insights from that discussion, providing SAP professionals with a deeper understanding of the 3SL conversion process, its benefits, and how to navigate the challenges effectively.

Watch the video below for a more interactive experience and to hear directly from the experts:

 

Introduction to 3SL Conversion

The session hosted by Yannick Peterschmitt kicked off with an introduction by Calin Cernea, product management expert from SAP's SaaS Foundation, who outlined the fundamental differences between the 2-system and 3-system landscapes. Traditionally, a 2-system landscape comprises quality and production environments. However, as businesses grow and their needs evolve, this setup may no longer suffice.

The 3-system landscape introduces a new test environment, which takes over the quality assurance tasks from the quality system, while the production environment remains unchanged. This addition is crucial for businesses looking to implement new projects without disruptions caused by system upgrades.

 Figure 1: 2-System Landscape to 3-System Landscape Conversion

Customer Insights: Making Science's Conversion Experience

Javier Mora, from Making Science, shared their company's journey of converting to a 3-system landscape. The primary motivation for this transition was to standardize business operations across various countries and minimize disruptions during system implementations. The 3-system landscape facilitated a smoother testing environment, allowing Making Science to configure their operations more effectively and with fewer interruptions.

Victor Alvarez, from the SAP partner Seidor, highlighted the collaborative effort between Making Science and Seidor in navigating the conversion process. From the early stages, Seidor was deeply involved, helping to elucidate the benefits of the new system landscape and managing the technical aspects of the migration. This partnership ensured that the conversion did not impact daily operations and integrated new functionalities seamlessly.

Preparation is key to a successful conversion. The discussion emphasized the importance of maintaining clear and consistent communication with SAP, leveraging detailed documentation, and holding regular alignment meetings. These practices help ensure that all parties are on the same page and that the conversion process proceeds with minimal disruptions.

Post-Conversion Activities and Best Practices

Post-conversion activities are critical to ensuring that the new system functions as intended. Calin from SAP explained that one of the primary goals of the 3SL/CBC conversion program is to minimize these activities, allowing customers to quickly resume normal operations. He recommended that customers engage with the available resources on the SAP community page, which includes how-to guides and videos that provide further clarity on post-conversion processes.

Find all relevant material in the SAP Three-System Landscape Community

The transition to a 3-system landscape in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers numerous benefits, including enhanced scalability, stability, and the ability to conduct thorough testing before production releases. As businesses continue to grow and face increasingly complex challenges, adopting a 3-system landscape can provide the flexibility and robustness needed to support continuous improvement and innovation.

Call to Action

For SAP professionals and customers interested in exploring the 3SL conversion further, the full Expert Talk video is an invaluable resource. Additionally, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 3-system landscape page on the SAP Community website offers extensive documentation and learning materials to support your conversion journey.

Engage with these resources to ensure a smooth and effective transition to the 3-system landscape, positioning your business for success in the dynamic digital landscape.

Take the chance to watch all our Expert Talk session available on YouTube.

 

 Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success Enablement & Adoption offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Unlocking Digital Excellence: Navigating SAP S/4HANA Transformation Strategically
entry.content:
So why did I use the 2003 legacy mobile phone the way I did? Because the limitations of the old technology did not allow me to work any different with it. The same is true with enterprises using the legacy technology. How they work with legacy ERP is determined by the guardrails and limitations of its 20th-century technology.

But approaching S/4 HANA (the 2024 smartphone example in my analogy) by migrating all the stuff from ECC into it means bringing over its limitations into a world where these limitations do not exist anymore. So we should approach SAP S/4 HANA the same way we approach smartphones in our private lives: Not backwards-looking but aligning the new capabilities with the challenges and needs of our situations and entirely using the aspects we will benefit most. By doing so, we will fully leverage the benefits of today's new technology capabilities.

For example, we are now experiencing a gradual shift to new sources of automotive fuel. Ten years ago, almost all cars ran on diesel and gasoline. Now, we see a electric vehicles on the road, but gas-powered cars will be nearly extinct in ten years. This will cause profound disruption that affects countless businesses. As the threat of climate change identified 50 years ago finally enters the public consciousness, the gradual shift will become sudden.

With today's ERP, you can't even talk seriously about innovation. It's like using a small shovel to mine for gold when you could be using an excavator. The excavator is SAP S/4HANA, the new ERP system with built-in intelligent technologies that are the foundation of a future-ready business.

Case-study and Business Challenge - How can the shipping Industry keep Pace with the Advancing Digital Transformation

With less than 40% of the commercial shipping fleet using even basic software services, you might think it is too premature to discuss digital transformation. However, change usually happens when no one is expecting it. Sea freight forwarding has stayed the same for too long as compared to land and air logistics and this is an opportune time for shipping to undergo a transformation to match customer expectations.

The old-fashioned business model of the shipping industry was centered on selling capacity alone, relying on time-consuming and sluggish processes like the handling of voluminous documentation and approvals.

Despite being an important cog in the wheel of supply chain solutions across the globe, shipping is yet to adopt the kind of digitization seen in the land-based logistics sector. One of the primary reasons for this was the connectivity issue and the highly fragmented nature of the segment.

Modernization and automation of port operations are among the factors that can drive port efficiency by effectively disseminating information and reducing paper-based manual workflows.

Building an SAP S/4HANA-based global business platform aligned with strategic goals

· More than 80% of all commercial cargo is transported via sea freight forwarding services; the digitization and visibility compared to other modes of transportation are very low. All this is set to change as technology enhancement allows better connectivity and taps opportunities that were missing earlier.

· Working with this knowledge, we began redesigning the Port Operation standard business platform based on SAP S/4 HANA. This design focused on business processes, IT architecture, and master data management, and it leveraged SAP CPI methodology, which introduced a range of standard business processes.

entry.title: How to delete amended record of timesheet for particular week into SuccessFactors?
entry.content:
I will not show how employee submit the timesheet and how approval process happens.

I will show the procedure directly, If employee tries to amend the approved timesheet then the original timesheet will be overridden and new timesheet record will be created.

In backend admin can only access these records using below navigation:

Manage data-> Employee timesheet

Example:

Employee has submitted the timesheet and it was approved.

First time System will generate one external code for a timesheet, if it’s amended by employee then it’s replaced with new external code

 

For example:

Employee had amended the approved timesheet and submitted for approval again:

Now the timesheet new external code generated by system and old record will be stored in replaces External code shown in below image.

In the backend the new External code will show as approved:

Replaced external code is old one and it will show as cancelled into backend, this replaced external code should be deleted from the system to avoid issues.

Replaces- 3fe750fcd3de4680a9c87dab52477b1e

To delete the replaces external code click on "permanently Delete entry" option.

Click on Yes to continue

After deletion of replaced external code system will show only one record/external code into trace for the particular week timesheet:

Note: 

If you want to do bulk deletion they you need to download the "employee timesheet" data via "Import and Export data" tool

then filter the cancelled external code and upload with "delete" operation.

Conclusion:

This type of issue comes when the employees are allowed to amend the timesheet after approval.

We should delete the replaced one and new one will reside into system with actual hours.

The deletion will avoid issue of multiple records in backend and we can keep only actual records for particular week.

entry.title: Recurring deduction future timeslice records mass deletion
entry.content:
Requirement:

Delete the future time slice data or pay components from recurring deductions portlet, we can use the below import process so that the a particular time slice record or a particular pay component will be removed from employees recurring deductions data.

Below sample employee has multiple records in recurring deductions, we must remove all pay component deductions which start from Jan.2026 for all employees.

Possible Solution:

Mass deletion of the required time slice and removing all deductions for employees can be done with the import.

Export data of existing employees recurring deductions with “Import and export data” with Generic object as “Recurring deduction” with including dependencies “Yes”.

  • To remove the deduction, pay components from future dated records –
  1. Open the file “Recurring Deduction-Recurring Items” to remove the Jan.2026 pay component records for the appropriate employees.
  2. Insert “DELIMIT” operation for the deductions which are not required as shown in below picture.
  3. Import the CSV file for generic object “Recurring Deduction-Recurring Items”.

 

  • To remove the Jan.2026 time slice for all the deductions available for user-

Open the file “Recurring Deduction.csv” this file will have all the employee recurring deduction records with employee number and the effective date of the deduction.

  1. Open the file “Recurring Deduction.csv” to remove the Jan.2026 time slice.
  2. Remove the timeslice records not required( as highlighted in below picture) or Insert “DELETE” operation for the deductions which are not required
  3. Import the CSV file for generic object “Recurring Deduction”.

Below screenshot shows the export file of recurringdeduction.csv

Below picture shows the import file which has the user records after removing the not required time slice.

For the below sample employee, Jan.2026 time slice is now removed from the employee recurring deductions portlet after the successful import.

This is how to delete Recurring Deductions from employee profile. Hope this blog is helpful.

entry.title: Re: SQL Anywhere on ARM (Apple Silicon)

As the tools we use are now arm64 based it's getting more and more complicated with an Intel based SQL Anywhere. Robert

entry.title: SAP Estende Suporte para Versões Antigas Até 2033
entry.content:
A Nova Estratégia da SAP para a Business Suite 7

A SAP decidiu que vai encerrar o suporte para a antiga Business Suite 7 e está pressionando seus clientes a atualizarem seus sistemas. No entanto, agora a empresa oferece uma alternativa para estender o uso desses sistemas – uma medida que beneficia tanto os clientes quanto a própria SAP.

Empresas que aderirem ao programa *Rise with SAP* poderão continuar utilizando seus sistemas antigos até o final de 2033. Originalmente, o suporte seria encerrado em 2027, e a manutenção estendida, que exige um custo adicional, seria encerrada em 2030. Isso significa que os clientes têm mais tempo para se adaptar, já que a migração para uma nova solução pode levar anos.

A SAP confirmou os planos e destacou que essa opção visa facilitar a transição para o *Rise with SAP*, garantindo a continuidade dos negócios de seus clientes. Essa nova alternativa é chamada de **SAP ERP, private edition, transition option**, mas mais detalhes, como os custos, só serão divulgados ao longo do primeiro semestre deste ano.

Apesar da prorrogação, a SAP deixou claro que o suporte para a Business Suite 7 terminará definitivamente em 2027. Para quem ainda não começou a transição, essa prorrogação pode ser um alívio, já que mais de 60% dos clientes ainda utilizam o ECC.

Em resumo: pague um pouco mais, respire até 2033 e tenha tempo para planejar a migração para a nova geração de produtos SAP.

Fonte:

https://www.handelsblatt.com/technik/it-internet/s-4hana-das-ist-saps-neue-strategie-zur-business-suite-7/100101152.html?paymentupdate=undefined
